Configurations
  • Editor
  • Neovim Configuration
  • Plugins
  • View page source

Plugins

This section documents the plugin system.

Overview

Plugins are managed using Neovim’s native package feature:

  • vim.pack (built-in, no external plugin manager)

This configuration intentionally avoids third-party managers such as lazy.nvim in favor of a simpler and more transparent setup.

All plugin definitions and their configuration are centralized in:

editor/nvim/lua/plugins.lua

Design

The plugin system follows these principles:

  • Single file configuration: all plugins and their setup live in one place

  • Reduced indirection: avoids splitting configuration across many files

  • Gradual minimization: plugins are used pragmatically and may be removed over time

This makes the setup easier to audit and refactor.

Plugin Installation

Plugins are declared using vim.pack.add:

vim.pack.add({
    { src = "https://github.com/user/plugin" },
})

Below is the current plugin list:

 1	{ src = "https://github.com/oskarnurm/koda.nvim" },                              -- light theme
 2	{ src = "https://github.com/metalelf0/black-metal-theme-neovim" },               -- dark theme
 3	{ src = "https://github.com/windwp/nvim-autopairs" },                            -- autopairs
 4	{ src = "https://github.com/stevearc/conform.nvim" },                            -- formatter
 5	{ src = "https://github.com/lewis6991/gitsigns.nvim" },                          -- git integration for buffers
 6	{ src = "https://github.com/onsails/lspkind.nvim" },                             -- pictograms
 7	{ src = "https://github.com/L3MON4D3/LuaSnip" },                                 -- snippets
 8	{ src = "https://github.com/nvim-mini/mini.icons" },                             -- icons
 9	{ src = "https://github.com/stevearc/oil.nvim" },                                -- file explorer
10	{ src = "https://github.com/nvim-lua/plenary.nvim" },                            -- dependency
11	{ src = "https://github.com/MeanderingProgrammer/render-markdown.nvim" },        -- inline md render
12	{ src = "https://github.com/SCJangra/table-nvim" },                              -- md table editting
13	{ src = "https://github.com/nvim-telescope/telescope-fzf-native.nvim" },         -- NOT WORKING
14	{ src = "https://github.com/nvim-telescope/telescope.nvim" },                    -- important
15	{ src = "https://github.com/folke/todo-comments.nvim" },                         -- highlight todo comments
16	{ src = "https://github.com/nvim-treesitter/nvim-treesitter",            version = "main" }, -- important
17	{ src = "https://github.com/nvim-treesitter/nvim-treesitter-textobjects" },      -- important
18	{ src = "https://github.com/lervag/vimtex" },                                    -- latex
19	{ src = "https://github.com/ej-shafran/compile-mode.nvim" },                     -- compile-mode
20	-- { src = "https://github.com/folke/which-key.nvim" }, -- Not working for me on nvim unstable

Categories

Plugins can be roughly grouped by their purpose:

UI / Appearance

  • koda.nvim (light theme)

  • black-metal-theme-neovim (dark theme)

  • mini.icons (icons)

  • lspkind.nvim (pictograms)

Editing

  • nvim-autopairs (automatic bracket pairing)

  • LuaSnip (snippets)

Navigation / Files

  • oil.nvim (file explorer)

  • telescope.nvim (fuzzy finder)

  • telescope-fzf-native.nvim (native fzf extension)

Language / Syntax

  • nvim-treesitter

  • nvim-treesitter-textobjects

  • vimtex (LaTeX support)

Development Tools

  • conform.nvim (formatting)

  • gitsigns.nvim (git integration)

  • todo-comments.nvim (highlight TODOs)

  • compile-mode.nvim (compilation interface)

Utilities / Dependencies

  • plenary.nvim (utility library)

  • render-markdown.nvim (inline markdown rendering)

  • table-nvim (markdown table editing)

Configuration

After installation, each plugin is configured within the same file.

Rationale:

  • Keeps all plugin-related logic in a single location

  • Reduces context switching

  • Makes dependencies and interactions easier to track

Note

The configuration section of plugins.lua is intentionally not fully reproduced here, as it is large and subject to frequent change.

Refer directly to the source file for implementation details.

Notes

  • Some plugins may be temporarily disabled or marked as not working.

  • Native features of Neovim are preferred over plugins when possible.

  • Over time, this setup aims to reduce reliance on external plugins.
